common_data_types.label: Common Data Types
common_data_types.summary: Learn the beginner-friendly uses of integers, text types, decimal-style numbers, and date/time-style columns in SQLite table design.

sketch1.title: When do you use text types?

sketch2.title: When do you use decimal numbers?

sketch3.title: How do beginners store dates and times?

integers.title: Integer columns are useful for whole-number values.
integers.bodyMarkdown:
A beginner often uses `INTEGER` for values like:

- ids
- counts
- ages
- grade levels

These are values that are usually whole numbers.

So the big idea is:

> integers are useful for whole-number data

text_types.title: Text columns are useful for words, labels, and descriptions.
text_types.bodyMarkdown:
A beginner often uses `TEXT` for values like:

- names
- titles
- cities
- emails
- status labels

So the key idea is:

> text types are useful when the value is mainly words or labels

decimal_numbers.title: Decimal-style numbers are useful for values like prices.
decimal_numbers.bodyMarkdown:
A beginner often uses `REAL` in SQLite for numeric values that can include decimals.

Examples include:

- prices
- ratings
- measurements

So the main idea is:

> decimal-style numeric columns are useful when the value is not always a whole number

dates_and_times.title: In beginner SQLite practice, dates and times are often stored as text-like values.
dates_and_times.bodyMarkdown:
SQLite beginners often store date-like values in `TEXT` columns using a clear format.

Examples include:

- `2026-04-05`
- `2026-04-05 14:30:00`

So the important idea is:

> date and time values are often stored as readable text in beginner SQLite work

m13_s02_create_scores_table.title: Use integer columns
m13_s02_create_scores_table.prompt:
The SQLite workspace is using the blank `ddl_blank` dataset.

Write a query that creates a table named `scores` with these columns:

- `id` as `INTEGER`
- `student_id` as `INTEGER`
- `score` as `INTEGER`

Then click **Run**.

help.concept: Integer columns fit whole-number values like ids and scores.
help.hint_1: Use `INTEGER` for all three columns in this table.
help.hint_2: Build the table normally, and make sure each column uses the whole-number type listed in the prompt.

m13_s02_create_scores_table.hint: Use INTEGER for the id-style and score-style columns.
m13_s02_create_scores_table.starterCode: -- Create a scores table using integer columns

m13_s02_create_profiles_table.title: Use text columns
m13_s02_create_profiles_table.prompt:
The dataset is blank.

Write a query that creates a table named `profiles` with these columns:

- `id` as `INTEGER`
- `full_name` as `TEXT`
- `city` as `TEXT`
- `email` as `TEXT`

Then run it.

help.concept: Text columns are a good fit for names, cities, and email-like values.
help.hint_2: Use `TEXT` for the word-based columns and keep the structure simple and readable.

m13_s02_create_profiles_table.hint: Use TEXT for the word-based columns.
m13_s02_create_profiles_table.starterCode: -- Create a profiles table using text columns

m13_s02_create_products_price_table.title: Use decimal-style numeric columns
m13_s02_create_products_price_table.prompt:
The dataset is blank.

Write a query that creates a table named `products` with these columns:

- `id` as `INTEGER`
- `name` as `TEXT`
- `price` as `REAL`
- `rating` as `REAL`

Then click **Run**.

help.concept: SQLite beginners often use `REAL` for decimal-style numeric values such as prices and ratings.
help.hint_2: Build the table with one integer id, one text name, and two decimal-style numeric columns using `REAL`.

m13_s02_create_products_price_table.hint: Use REAL for the decimal-style columns.
m13_s02_create_products_price_table.starterCode: -- Create a products table using REAL for decimal-style values

m13_s02_create_events_date_table.title: Use date-like text columns
m13_s02_create_events_date_table.prompt:
The dataset is blank.

Write a query that creates a table named `events` with these columns:

- `id` as `INTEGER`
- `title` as `TEXT`
- `event_date` as `TEXT`
- `created_at` as `TEXT`

Then run it.

help.concept: In beginner SQLite design, date-like values are often stored in text columns using a readable format.
help.hint_2: Build the table with one id column and three text-based columns, including the two date-like ones.

m13_s02_create_events_date_table.hint: Use TEXT for the date-like columns in this beginner example.
m13_s02_create_events_date_table.starterCode: -- Create an events table using TEXT for date-like values

m13_s02_data_types_project.title: Practice common data types
m13_s02_data_types_project.prompt:
Use the SQL workspace on the right to practice data types on the blank `ddl_blank` dataset.

For each task:

1. write the table definition carefully
2. click **Run**
3. open the **Tables** tab to inspect the columns
4. check whether each type fits the intended value

Tasks:

1. Create a `scores` table using integers
2. Create a `profiles` table using text columns
3. Create a `products` table with decimal-style columns
4. Create an `events` table with date-like text columns
5. Create a `payments` table with `id`, `customer_name`, `amount`, and `paid_at`

Run one query at a time.
This project is about matching common data types to the right kind of data.

help.concept: The main skill is matching the kind of value to the most beginner-friendly type.
help.hint_1: Think whole number, words, decimal, or date-like text.
help.hint_2: Solve the tasks one by one. For each column, ask what kind of value it stores first, then choose the fitting type.

m13_s02_data_types_project.hint: Ask what kind of value the column stores before choosing its type.
m13_s02_data_types_project.starterCode:
-- Task 1
-- Create a scores table using integers

m13_s02_integers_meaning.title: When are integers useful?
m13_s02_integers_meaning.prompt: What is the main beginner use of an integer column?

help.concept: Integer columns fit whole-number values like ids and counts.
help.hint_2: These columns are useful when the value is not normally a decimal.

m13_s02_integers_meaning.hint: Think ids, counts, and other whole numbers.

options.a: For whole-number values like ids or counts
options.d: For every column no matter what

m13_s02_text_meaning.title: When are text columns useful?
m13_s02_text_meaning.prompt: What is the main beginner use of a text column?

help.concept: Text columns are useful for words, names, titles, and labels.
help.hint_2: A text column is a good fit when the data is mainly human-readable words or labels.

options.a: For names, titles, labels, and similar word-based values

m13_s02_decimal_meaning.title: When are decimal-style numbers useful?
m13_s02_decimal_meaning.prompt: What is the main beginner use of a decimal-style numeric column like `REAL`?

help.concept: REAL is useful when the value may include a decimal, such as a price.
help.hint_2: It is used when the number is not always a whole integer.

m13_s02_decimal_meaning.hint: Think prices and other non-whole-number values.

options.a: For values like prices that may include decimals

m13_s02_dates_meaning.title: How do beginners often store dates in SQLite?
m13_s02_dates_meaning.prompt: How are date-like values often stored in beginner SQLite practice?

help.concept: Beginner SQLite work often stores date-like values as text in a readable format.
help.hint_2: The key beginner idea is readable date-style text rather than a special advanced type.

m13_s02_dates_meaning.hint: Think readable date strings in TEXT columns.

options.a: As text-like date strings in TEXT columns
options.d: Dates cannot be stored in beginner SQLite work

m13_s02_type_fitting_meaning.title: What is the main goal of choosing a fitting type?
m13_s02_type_fitting_meaning.prompt: What is the main idea behind choosing a fitting data type for each column?

help.concept: A fitting type helps the table match the meaning of the stored value.
help.hint_2: The more the type fits the intended value, the clearer the design becomes.

m13_s02_type_fitting_meaning.hint: Think match the type to the value meaning.

options.a: Make the column type fit the kind of value being stored
options.b: Use the same type for every column
options.c: Avoid thinking about the data at all
options.d: Replace clear naming with random types

m13_s02_integers_true.title: What is true about integers?
m13_s02_integers_true.prompt: Select all true statements about integer columns in beginner table design.

help.concept: Integer columns are a natural fit for whole-number data.
help.hint_2: Choose the statements about whole-number storage.

options.c: Ages can often use integer columns
options.d: Integers are the best fit for every product name
options.e: Integers are useful when decimals are not needed

m13_s02_text_true.title: What is true about text columns?
m13_s02_text_true.prompt: Select all true statements about text columns in beginner table design.

help.concept: Text columns are useful for readable words and labels.
help.hint_2: Choose the statements about word-based values.

options.b: Email-like values often use text columns
options.c: City names often use text columns
options.d: Text columns are never useful for labels
options.e: Text columns are useful for word-based values

m13_s02_decimals_true.title: What is true about decimal-style numeric columns?
m13_s02_decimals_true.prompt: Select all true statements about decimal-style numeric columns like REAL.

help.concept: REAL is a beginner-friendly fit for numbers that may include decimal parts.
help.hint_2: Choose the statements about values that are not always whole numbers.

options.a: Prices often use decimal-style numeric columns
options.b: Ratings can use decimal-style numeric columns
options.c: Measurements can use decimal-style numeric columns
options.d: REAL is only for table names
options.e: Decimal-style types help when whole numbers are not enough

m13_s02_dates_true.title: What is true about dates and times in beginner SQLite design?
m13_s02_dates_true.prompt: Select all true statements about dates and times in beginner SQLite table design.

help.concept: Beginner SQLite lessons often store date-like values in readable text columns.
help.hint_2: Choose the statements about date-style values being stored clearly.

m13_s02_dates_true.hint: Think readable date strings in text columns.

options.a: Date-like values are often stored as TEXT in beginner SQLite work
options.b: Readable date formats can make beginner tables easier to inspect
options.c: A created_at column can be a TEXT column in beginner practice
options.d: Dates can never be stored as text in SQLite
options.e: This is a common beginner-safe design choice

m13_s02_data_types_workflow_true.title: What matches a good beginner workflow for choosing data types?
m13_s02_data_types_workflow_true.prompt: Select all statements that match a good beginner workflow for choosing data types.

help.concept: A good workflow is to identify what kind of value a column holds, then choose the simplest fitting type.
help.hint_2: Choose the steps that help match types to meanings.

m13_s02_data_types_workflow_true.hint: Identify the kind of value first, then choose the fitting type.

options.a: Ask what kind of value the column stores
options.b: Use integers for whole-number fields when appropriate
options.c: Use text for word-based values when appropriate
options.d: Use decimal-style numeric types for values like prices
options.e: Ignore what the values are actually supposed to represent
